title: Hardware Warranty Expiry
spl:
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:cmdb_ci" operational_status="operational"
| eval w_end=strptime(warranty_expires,"%Y-%m-%d")
| where isnotnull(w_end) AND w_end < relative_time(now(),"+60d@d") AND w_end > now()
| eval days_left=round((w_end-now())/86400,0)
| table name, serial_number, warranty_expires, days_left, support_group
| sort days_left
description: Tracks warranty end dates for hardware CIs to avoid unsupported break-fix gaps and budget surprises.
value: Tracks warranty end dates for hardware CIs to avoid unsupported break-fix gaps and budget surprises.
implementation: Map OEM warranty fields from procurement or discovery. Alert at 60/30 days. Exclude disposed assets via `install_status`.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_snow`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=snow:cmdb_ci` (`warranty_expires`, `u_warranty_end`).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Map OEM warranty fields from procurement or discovery. Alert at 60/30 days. Exclude disposed assets via `install_status`.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:cmdb_ci" operational_status="operational"
| eval w_end=strptime(warranty_expires,"%Y-%m-%d")
| where isnotnull(w_end) AND w_end < relative_time(now(),"+60d@d") AND w_end > now()
| eval days_left=round((w_end-now())/86400,0)
| table name, serial_number, warranty_expires, days_left, support_group
| sort days_left
```

Understanding this SPL

**Hardware Warranty Expiry** — Tracks warranty end dates for hardware CIs to avoid unsupported break-fix gaps and budget surprises.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=snow:cmdb_ci` (`warranty_expires`, `u_warranty_end`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_snow`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: itsm; **sourcetype**: snow:cmdb_ci. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=itsm, sourcetype="snow:cmdb_ci". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **w_end** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnotnull(w_end) AND w_end < relative_time(now(),"+60d@d") AND w_end > now()` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **days_left** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Pipeline stage (see **Hardware Warranty Expiry**): table name, serial_number, warranty_expires, days_left, support_group
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (warranty expiring), Timeline (expiry by month), Single value (CIs <30d).
visualization: Table (warranty expiring), Timeline (expiry by month), Single value (CIs <30d).
